title: euthamia tenuifolia var. pycnocephala
intro: Euthamia tenuifolia var. pycnocephala, also known as the bitter goldenrod, is a perennial plant species native to North America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The bitter goldenrod has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the relief of respiratory problems such as asthma and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, Euthamia tenuifolia var. pycnocephala is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
